purpose: This policy describes how <<LAB_NAME>> receives, stores, and supports release of HCT/P tissue products. Required by 21 CFR 1271 (HCT/Ps), 21 CFR 1271.155-170 (donor eligibility), and 42 CFR 493.1232 (specimen integrity).

cfr_text_blocks.label: FDA: Donor screening (general)
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: You must screen a donor of cells or tissue by reviewing the donor's relevant medical records for risk factors for, and clinical evidence of, relevant communicable disease agents and diseases.

scope: <<LAB_NAME>> personnel receiving, storing, or releasing transplant tissue products.
policy_statements: Tissue products are received with complete donor eligibility documentation per 21 CFR 1271.155-170; products missing eligibility evidence are not accepted.
Storage follows the tissue product's specific requirements (temperature, container, light); storage is monitored continuously with alarmed devices.
Release to surgical use requires verified donor eligibility, intact packaging, valid expiration, and recipient/product identification matching the request.
Adverse events related to tissue products are reported to the supplier and to FDA per 21 CFR 1271.350 (HCT/P deviation reporting).
Records retained per 21 CFR 1271.270 (10 years past distribution) and 42 CFR 493.1105.
procedure_steps: On receipt, verify donor eligibility documentation, packaging integrity, expiration, and storage history during transport.
Store per the product's requirements with alarmed monitoring.
On release, verify recipient/product match, eligibility, packaging, and expiration; document.
Report adverse events to the supplier and FDA per regulation; document.
definitions: Departure from manufacturing requirements, applicable regulations, or established specifications for HCT/P products. Reportable to FDA per 21 CFR 1271.350.
